Queries
======
1. Customer List: Retrieve the name and city of all customers.

2. Stock Check: Find the names of medicines that are 'Syrup' or 'Injection'

3. High Value Bills: List all orders where the total amount is greater than 500

4. Mumbai Residents: Find the phone numbers of all customers living in

'Mumbai'.

Level 2: Intermediate Queries (Aggregates & Joins)

5. Payment Analysis: How many orders were paid using 'UPI'?

6. Supplier Count: Count how many medicines are supplied by 'Apollo Distributors (Use Subquery or Join).

7. Order Details: List the Customer Name, Order Date, and Total Amount for every order placed.

8. Revenue by Mode: Calculate the total money earned through each payment mode (UPI, Card, Cash).
9. Get the most selling medicine
10. Get the Customers who have spent more than 1000

mysql> -- we need to figure out on what day of week most users register on
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.000 sec)

mysql> select dayname(created_at) as day_of_week,count(*) as total_count from users group by day_of_week order by total_count desc;
+-------------+-------------+
| day_of_week | total_count |
+-------------+-------------+
| Thursday    |          16 |
| Sunday      |          16 |
| Friday      |          15 |
| Tuesday     |          14 |
| Monday      |          14 |
| Wednesday   |          13 |
| Saturday    |          12 |

mysql> select u.id,u.username,u.created_at
    -> from users u left join photos p 
    -> on u.id=p.id
    -> where p.user_id is null;
Empty set (0.001 sec)

mysql> select u.id,username,u.created_at from users u left join photos p on u.id=p.id where p.user_id is null;
Empty set (0.000 sec)

mysql> -- most likes on a single photo
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.000 sec)

mysql> select users.id as user_id,username,photos.id as photo_id,photos.image_url
    -> ,photos.image_url,count(*) as total_likes_count
    -> from photos join likes
    -> on photos.id=likes.photo
    -> _id
    -> \c
mysql> select users.id as user_id,username,photos.id as photo_id,photos.image_url ,photos.image_url,count(*) as total_likes_count from photos join likes on photos.id=likes.photo_id join users on users.id=photos.user_id group by photos.id order by total_likes_count desc;

mysql> select users.id as user_id,username,photos.id as photo_id,photos.image_url ,photos.image_url,count(*) as total_likes_count from photos join likes on photos.id=likes.photo_id join users on users.id=photos.user_id group by photos.id order by total_likes_count desc limit 1;

| user_id | username      | photo_id | image_url           | image_url           | total_likes_count |
+---------+---------------+----------+---------------------+---------------------+-------------------+
|      52 | Zack_Kemmer93 |      145 | https://jarret.name | https://jarret.name |                48 |
+---------+---------------+----------+---------------------+---------------------+-------------------+

    ->     expiry_date DATE,
    ->     supplier_id INT,
    ->     FOREIGN KEY (supplier_id) REFERENCES suppliers(supplier_id)
    -> );
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.007 sec)

mysql> 
mysql> CREATE TABLE orders (
    ->     order_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    ->     customer_id INT,
    ->     order_date DATE,
    ->     total_amount DECIMAL(10,2),
    ->     payment_mode ENUM('UPI', 'Cash', 'Card'),
    ->     FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id)
    -> );
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.007 sec)

mysql> 
mysql> CREATE TABLE order_items (
    ->     item_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    ->     order_id INT,
    ->     medicine_id INT,
    ->     quantity INT,
    ->     subtotal DECIMAL(10,2),
    ->     FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES orders(order_id),
    ->     FOREIGN KEY (medicine_id) REFERENCES medicines(medicine_id)
    -> );
